methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $97M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Nomagic, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kacper Nowicki, has raised $97M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private firms in the robotics and manufacturing sector at similar stages. The private peer set includes 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $97M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yields $184M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity within frontier robotics; 1X Technologies and Bedrock Robotics anchor the high end at $10.0B and $1.8B respectively, while Collaborative Robotics and Fairmat sit at $500M and $350M.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $157M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $630M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty regarding Nomagic's competitive positioning within a volatile sector. Key variables include progress toward product commercialization, differentiation relative to better-capitalized peers, and market timing for next-round exit or financing.
